Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.

Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The 4th DOWN was blocked again (only 2 cells changed, HUD). The blue is at rows 48-51, cols 20-39 after 3 successful DOWNs.
So the blue can only reach rows 48-51 regardless of horizontal position. This means there's a boundary at rows 52-55 that blocks the blue.
Let me check: the magenta is at rows 56-59, cols 40-43. The blue is 4 rows tall. If the blue is at rows 48-51, its bottom edge is at row 51. The magenta's top edge is at row 56. So there's a 4-row gap (rows 52-55).
But the blue can't move further DOWN. This suggests there's an invisible boundary or the orange area has a lower limit.
Wait, looking at the segmentation, the orange area is rows 4-62. The blue is within the orange area. But the blue can only reach rows 48-51. This is strange.
Actually, maybe the boundary is at row 52, and the blue can't go beyond row 51 (since it's 4 rows tall, rows 48-51 means the bottom is at row 51). But why would there be a boundary at row 52?
Let me look at the image more carefully. The green bar is at row 63 (the N bar). The charcoal is at rows 60-62. The magenta is at rows 56-59. The orange area is rows 4-62.
Hmm, maybe the blue can't go below row 51 because there's an invisible boundary. Or maybe the blue's movement is limited by the level design.
Let me try a different approach. Instead of trying to match Level 1's solution, let me explore what SPACE does at different positions.
I've tried SPACE at:
1. rows 36-39, cols 36-55 (RIGHT x4) - no impact
2. rows 48-51, cols 36-55 (RIGHT x4, DOWN x3) - no impact
3. rows 48-51, cols 28-47 (RIGHT x4, DOWN x3, LEFT x2) - no impact
4. rows 12-15, cols 28-47 (RIGHT x4, DOWN x3, LEFT x2, UP x8) - no impact
5. rows 12-15, cols 24-43 (RIGHT x4, DOWN x3, LEFT x2, UP x8, LEFT x1) - GAME OVER
The GAME OVER was triggered when the blue was at rows 12-15, cols 24-43. This is the same position as Level 1's winning position! But in Level 2, this position causes game over.
Wait, maybe the game over is because I ran out of moves (the N bar reached 0). Let me check the N bar.
Actually, the N bar is at 64 cells (full) after the level restarted. So the game over wasn't due to running out of moves.
Let me reconsider. The game over might be triggered when the blue is in a "wrong" position and SPACE is pressed. In Level 1, the winning position was rows 12-15, cols 24-43. In Level 2, this position causes game over.
So the winning position in Level 2 is different from Level 1. Let me think about what makes Level 2 different.
In Level 1, the charcoal/magenta were at the top (rows 1-7, cols 36-39). In Level 2, they're at the bottom (rows 56-62, cols 40-43).
In Level 1, the blue was moved UP to be near the charcoal/magenta. In Level 2, maybe the blue should be moved DOWN to be near the charcoal/magenta.
But the blue can only reach rows 48-51, which is 5 rows above the magenta at rows 56-59.
Let me try SPACE at rows 48-51, cols 20-39 (the current position).
